frameworks.name: temporal receptive windows
frameworks.one_line: cortical regions integrate sensory information over region-specific temporal extents

frameworks.name: cortical hierarchy of receptive fields
frameworks.one_line: extends spatial receptive-field hierarchy to the temporal domain

core_question: Does the human cortex contain a hierarchy of regions tuned to progressively longer temporal scales of accumulated sensory information?
key_assumptions: response reliability across repeated viewings indexes sensitivity to a given temporal scale
scrambling order disrupts information at scales longer than the scramble segment
fMRI BOLD reliability can dissociate short- vs long-timescale integration
manipulations: piecewise temporal scrambling at varying segment lengths
repetition across viewings to measure response reliability
key_findings: early visual areas (V1, MT+) respond reliably regardless of temporal disruption
higher areas (STS, precuneus, LS, TPJ, FEF) respond reliably only to intact forward films
LS, TPJ, FEF require integration over ~36 s while STS and precuneus require ~12 s
interpretations: the cortex implements a hierarchy of progressively longer temporal receptive windows
this temporal hierarchy parallels the well-known hierarchy of spatial receptive fields
